TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON OCT-15-2018 IN 206, PLAINTIFF SUZANNE GAMBLE'S MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE WAS HEARD. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-30-2020 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-31-2019 Set For Case Management Conference On Feb-19-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-16-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tim Fama, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, T:415.554.3921, for defendant/moving party;
Suzanne Gamble, T:415.724.7666, plaintiff/opposing party, appearing on her own behalf.

RULING: Plaintiff contested the tentative ruling. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Summary Judgment, as follows:

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant filed its motion on July 19, 2019, on more than the statutorily-required 75 days notice. (Code Civ. Proc. 437c(a)(2).) On August 2, 2019, for good cause shown and pursuant to the agreement of the parties, the Court ordered that the motion be heard on October 16, 2019, the originally noticed hearing date, less than 30 days prior to the November 4, 2019 trial date. (Code Civ. Proc. 437c(a)(3).) That order provided that Plaintiff's opposition papers would be due 14 calendar days before the hearing date, pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. 437c(b)(3). Plaintiff has not opposed the motion for summary judgment on its merits or filed a separate statement of material facts in opposition to the motion. Plaintiff is not entitled to special consideration because of her self-represented status. (See ViaView, Inc. v. Retzlaff (2016) 1 Cal.App.5th 198, 208.) Accordingly, the motion must be granted.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
